report_metadata.title: 2026 Global Threat Report: Year of the Evasive Adversary
report_metadata.campaign_name: Not applicable; this is an annual global threat report covering multiple campaigns and trends.
report_metadata.attributed_actor: Multiple actors including SCATTERED SPIDER, BLOCKADE SPIDER, PUNK SPIDER, CHATTY SPIDER, PRESSURE CHOLLIMA, FAMOUS CHOLLIMA, COZY BEAR, FANCY BEAR, IMPERIAL KITTEN, GRACEFUL SPIDER, VICE SPIDER, and various PANDA (China-nexus) and CHOLLIMA (DPRK-nexus) groups.

iocs.context: Exploited by Cerber ransomware operators against Langflow AI to deploy ransomware.
SQL injection vulnerability exploited by OPERATOR PANDA for initial access and RCE.
File upload vulnerability exploited by PHANTOM PANDA.
React2Shell insecure deserialization vulnerability exploited by VAULT PANDA and GENESIS PANDA.
Part of an exploit chain used by WARP PANDA against VPN appliances for initial access.
Part of an exploit chain used by WARP PANDA against VPN appliances for initial access.
Exploited by HOLLOW PANDA against Check Point VPN appliances.
Command injection vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS exploited by OPERATOR PANDA.
Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) RCE vulnerability exploited by VEILED PANDA.
Zero-day vulnerability exploited by GRACEFUL SPIDER for data exfiltration.
Zero-day LPE vulnerability in Microsoft Windows Common Log File System (CLFS) driver exploited by VICE SPIDER.

mitre_mappings.report_context: CHATTY SPIDER and SCATTERED SPIDER used voice phishing (vishing) campaigns to persuade employees to install software or reset passwords.
CHATTY SPIDER persuaded victims to download and install remote monitoring and management (RMM) tooling.

mitre_mappings.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Exfiltration Over Unencrypted/Obfuscated Non-C2 Protocol
mitre_mappings.report_context: CHATTY SPIDER pivoted to using Google Drive for data exfiltration after an initial attempt using WinSCP was blocked.

mitre_mappings.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
mitre_mappings.report_context: SCATTERED SPIDER leveraged unmanaged virtual machines, which lack EDR or security tooling, to perform credential dumping.
SCATTERED SPIDER mounted a domain controller's virtual disk to an unmanaged VM to copy the ntds.dit file.
PUNK SPIDER conducted remote file encryption by executing ransomware on an unmanaged host and targeting files on remote SMB shares.
Ransomware adversaries like PUNK SPIDER and SCATTERED SPIDER deployed ransomware to encrypt files on both managed hosts (via remote encryption) and VMware ESXi systems.

mitre_mappings.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
mitre_mappings.report_context: PRESSURE CHOLLIMA compromised Safe{Wallet} by delivering trojanized software, and another actor compromised the Notepad++ update process.

mitre_mappings.technique_name: Hijack Execution Flow: DLL Search Order Hijacking
mitre_mappings.report_context: HOLLOW PANDA deployed ShadowPad malware via DLL search-order hijacking on compromised Check Point VPN appliances.
eCrime and state-nexus actors (IMPERIAL KITTEN, COZY BEAR) leveraged AiTM phishing kits to intercept authentication flows and gain access to Microsoft 365 accounts.
COZY BEAR's phishing campaigns prompted users through legitimate Microsoft login pages, including OAuth 2.0 authorization code and device code flows, to capture session tokens.
Multiple adversaries gained initial access to cloud environments using compromised credentials obtained through phishing, vishing, or other means.
BLOCKADE SPIDER registered a new federated identity provider in a victim's Entra ID tenant to maintain persistent access.
BLOCKADE SPIDER created mail forwarding and deletion rules in Microsoft 365 to exfiltrate data and hide security alerts from legitimate users.
Threat actors used AI to accelerate malware development, citing ransomware variants FunkLocker and RALord generated by WormGPT.
Associated with IOC CVE-2025-3248
Associated with IOC CVE-2025-32706

paths.description: Adversary uses vishing to gain credentials, then leverages unmanaged systems for defense evasion to dump credentials and deploy ransomware on virtualization infrastructure.
State-nexus actors exploit public-facing edge devices, deploy malware via hijacking techniques, and establish covert C2 for long-term intelligence collection.
Adversary uses AiTM phishing to bypass MFA and steal session tokens, gaining access to cloud accounts for data collection and discovery.
Threat actor compromises a software developer or repository to distribute trojanized software to downstream victims.
